title: Chapter 1: Introduction to Philosophy
content: Philosophy begins with wonder. It is the rational attempt to formulate, understand, and answer fundamental questions.

keywords.Philosophy: The love of wisdom and the search for self-understanding.

title: Basic Features of Philosophy
content: Philosophy is characterized by its critical and systematic nature.

keywords.Critical Thinking: A logical process of analyzing and evaluating information.
keywords.Reflective: Examining our lives and the world around us deeply.
keywords.Independence: Thinking for oneself rather than following the crowd.

title: The Four Core Branches (Pillars)
content: Modern philosophy is divided into four major areas of study.

keywords.Metaphysics: The study of reality—what is real? (Existence, God, Mind).
keywords.Epistemology: The study of knowledge—how do we know? (Truth, Belief).
keywords.Axiology: The study of values—Ethics (Moral) and Aesthetics (Beauty).
keywords.Logic: The study of correct reasoning and arguments.

title: Importance of Learning Philosophy
content: Why is this course mandatory for all Ethiopian freshman students?

keywords.Intellectual Growth: Expanding your mind and reasoning skills.
keywords.Open-mindedness: Being willing to consider different perspectives.
keywords.Social Harmony: Understanding values to live better with others.

title: The Meaning of Logic
content: Logic is the heart of philosophy. It provides the 'rules' for thinking.

keywords.Goal of Logic: To develop methods for distinguishing good arguments from bad ones.
keywords.Reasoning: The process of drawing conclusions from facts or evidence.

title: Components of an Argument
content: In logic, an argument is a structure of ideas.

keywords.Statement: A sentence that has a truth-value (either True or False).
keywords.Premise: The evidence/reason given for a claim.
keywords.Conclusion: The main claim that is supported by the premises.

title: Argument Indicators (Very Important!)
content: How to identify premises and conclusions in a text.

keywords.Premise Indicators: Since, because, for, as, as indicated by, given that.
keywords.Conclusion Indicators: Therefore, thus, so, hence, consequently, it follows that.

title: Two Types of Reasoning
content: Arguments are classified based on the level of certainty they claim.

keywords.Deductive Argument: The conclusion follows with 100% necessity. (From General to Specific).
keywords.Inductive Argument: The conclusion follows with probability. (From Specific to General).

title: Evaluating Deductive Arguments
content: We check two things: Logic and Truth.

keywords.Validity: The structure is correct (If premises are true, conclusion MUST be true).
keywords.Soundness: The argument is Valid AND all the premises are actually True.

title: Evaluating Inductive Arguments
content: Since they are based on probability, we use different terms.

keywords.Strength: The premises make the conclusion very likely to be true.
keywords.Cogency: The argument is Strong AND all the premises are actually True.
